hints.text: Plates long or short w/ **blue strip left** (Euro-like); **taxis = deep-red plates**

hints.uniq_note: The Arabic+French mix is the Middle-East tell for Lebanon — other Arabic countries on Street View pair with English (UAE) or nothing (Jordan).

hints.text: **Only Middle-Eastern country with official shitcam** coverage; everything else = **Gen3 trekkers**
**Metal mesh poles**, sometimes painted bright **yellow**; generic wooden poles too

shared_with.note: Looks very similar to Jerusalem stone.
shared_with.diff: Hebrew → Israel; French/English mix + shitcam → Lebanon.

hints.text: Coverage concentrated on the **west coast north of Beirut**
**Zahle**: flat farmland w/ mountains NW (+ visible **black car** in/around Zahle); **NE mountains** east of Tripoli = snow coverage; **northern Lebanon** = very flat farmland, mountains SE
Only covered city with **modern high-rises**; promenade coverage: grey brick pavement + many **fan palms**; Université Saint Joseph = grand old sandstone building

**University spotlights**: Beirut Arab Univ. (Debbieh) = sparse modern sandstone on forested hill, sea far west; LIU = stone buildings w/ unpainted ground floors + beige upper floors; Balamand = cubic modern white; Lebanese American Univ. (E of Byblos) = urban, much construction

**Historic spotlights**: Byblos = ancient city, uniform unpainted stone; Bsharri trekker = big canyon south; **Baalbek** = Roman ruin complex; Mseilha fortress (SW of Tripoli) on a limestone monolith; the only rural coastal trekker road = SW of Tripoli
